scenario_name: Marketing content generator with brand-voice tuning and web-fetch tool
description: A marketing team deploys a content-generation assistant that drafts blog posts, ad copy, and social posts in the brand's tuned voice. The assistant has a web-fetch tool to gather contemporary references (competitor pages, trend articles, news). Generated content is reviewed by a marketing editor before publication. Risks span IP/copyright, brand reputation, hallucinated factual claims, and indirect prompt injection via fetched web content.
assumptions: Hosted LLM under enterprise data-isolation contract.
Generated content reviewed by a marketing editor before publication.
Brand-voice adapter is firm-owned and considered competitive IP.

components.description: Brand-voice adapter trained on the firm's prior approved content.
Web-fetch tool reading public competitor and news pages.
Brand-safety classifier (off-brand language); fact-claim detector; injection-classifier on fetched content.
Per-draft audit log capturing prompts, fetched sources, and final reviewer outcome.

risks_identified.rationale: Brand-voice adapter trained on copyrighted firm content; risk of memorizing competitor content scraped during research.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Training corpus restricted to firm-owned content; web-fetch limited to in-session use, not training.

risks_identified.rationale: Web-fetch tool brings adversary-controlled content into model context; indirect injection straight into draft outputs.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Injection-classifier on fetched content; reviewer gate before publication.

risks_identified.rationale: Hallucinated factual claims published as authoritative; reputational and regulatory risk.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Fact-claim detector flags unverifiable claims; reviewer must cite sources.

risks_identified.rationale: Brand-voice adapter is competitive IP; extraction via query patterns possible.
risks_identified.residual_rationale: Per-team rate caps; anomaly detection partial.

risks_identified.rationale: Marketing throughput depends on assistant; not a critical-availability service.
Copyright exposure from web-fetch; advertising-disclosure regulations on AI-generated content (FTC, Italy's AI labeling, EU AI Act transparency).
Audit logs not particularly sensitive but contain brand-strategy hints.

existing_controls.description: Brand-voice adapter trained only on firm-owned content.
Injection-classifier on fetched web content; flagged fetches held for human review.
Reviewer gate before publication; reviewer must cite sources for factual claims.
Per-team rate caps on adapter queries.
AI-generated content labeled per FTC and EU AI Act transparency requirements.

control_gaps.description: Web-fetch tool's allowed-domain list is permissive (general news allowed).
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Tighten the domain allowlist to a curated set of verified news and competitive-intel sources.
Run extractable-data audit on the adapter against the training corpus.

control_gaps.description: AI-disclosure labeling is end-of-process; in-draft signaling absent for reviewers.
control_gaps.suggested_remediation: Mark AI-generated sections inside the editorial UI so reviewers can apply heightened scrutiny.
